page: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a memory-optimized, bare-metal server instance powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and EBS optimization.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core and memory capabilities. The r6in.metal achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU stress tests, Passmark CPU scores, and extended instruction sets, including encryption. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 6700.59 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 338.95 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks show strong performance, processing 111.35 million operations per second.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, supporting high-throughput data transfers. However, the lack of local storage requires external EBS volumes for all storage needs, which may introduce latency tradeoffs compared to local NVMe. The hardware profile and benchmark results make this server suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring high memory density and substantial multi-core compute capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but contains no local storage or GPUs. The instance is highly optimized for network and EBS throughput, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the r6in.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads reaching 338.95 GB/sec. It also excels in database operations, processing 111.35 million Redis SET operations per second. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and robust multi-threaded capabilities, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a memory-optimized bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for large databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps networking, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring high-speed DDR4 RAM and 200 Gbps networking for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
1024.0 GB of high-speed DDR4 memory (8.0 GB per core)
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU resources included
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and enterprise applications
